rxn_class_summary: External-oxidant-free electrochemical [3+2] cycloaddition of N-sulfonylcyclopropanes with olefins via N-centered radical intermediates to access di- and tri-substituted cyclopentane analogs.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ferrocene to ferrocenium (Cp2Fe+) which acts as a single-electron mediator to oxidize the sulfonamide anion (generated by K2CO3 deprotonation of N-H sulfonamide) to an N-centered radical; cathode reduces a downstream distonic/imine radical intermediate to a carbanion that undergoes 5-exo cyclization.

key_intermediates.name: Ring-opened distonic carbon radical / N-sulfonyl imine with pendant C-radical (C)
Protonation by-product imine F (off-pathway, detected by HRMS [M+H]+ 298.1113)

electron_flow: At the anode Cp2Fe is oxidized to Cp2Fe+, which performs outer-sphere SET on the K2CO3-derived sulfonamide anion to generate an N-centered radical; this NCR triggers β-scission of the cyclopropane to a primary C-radical that adds to the alkene, and the resulting radical is reduced at the cathode to a carbanion that undergoes 5-exo cyclization onto the N-sulfonyl imine followed by protonation to deliver the cyclopentane.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Complete shutdown of the reaction at 2.0 equiv, supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene)
probe_reagents.observation: Complete shutdown of the reaction at 2.0 equiv, consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: N-cyclopropylacetamide (4, R=COCH3 instead of tosyl)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction, showing that the N-sulfonyl group is essential (acetyl cannot generate the requisite aza-anion / NCR).

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product after 1 h with starting materials largely recovered; rules out a chain-propagation mechanism and confirms continuous electron input is required.

probe_reagents.name: (3-Bromoprop-1-en-2-yl)benzene (allyl bromide radical clock/trap, 2o)
probe_reagents.observation: HRMS detection of trapped intermediate F ([M+H]+ 328.1371); hydrolysis liberated p-toluenesulfonamide confirmed by 1H NMR, supporting the proposed anionic/imine intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a with/without Cp2Fe and K2CO3
probe_reagents.observation: Ep(1a) = 2.08 V vs Ag/AgCl shifts slightly to 2.24 V with ferrocene, and a marked enhancement in catalytic current is observed with Cp2Fe and K2CO3, confirming electrocatalytic mediation by ferrocene.

notes: An alternative closure is proposed in which radical D adds directly to the N-sulfonyl imine and the resulting N-centered radical is cathodically reduced and protonated to give 3; the carbanion E can also be quenched by a proton to give acyclic imine F (HRMS-detected), accounting for moderate yields. SCXRD of 3ag (CCDC 2115609) confirms product connectivity/stereochemistry.
